diagrams.title: Pointer Arithmetic Memory Layout
diagrams.description: Shows how pointer arithmetic navigates through an integer array in memory
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how adding integers to pointers scales by data type size and accesses different array elements

diagrams.title: Pointer Subtraction Example
diagrams.description: Demonstrates pointer subtraction returning number of elements between two pointers
elements: p1 - p2 = -3 (negative when first pointer is lower)
diagrams.purpose: Understand that pointer subtraction gives element count, not byte difference

diagrams.title: Pre vs Post Increment on Pointers
elements: Case 1: *p returns 10, p still points to arr[0]
Case 2: *p++ returns 10, then p moves to arr[1]
Case 4: *++p moves p to arr[1] then returns *p (20)
diagrams.purpose: Clarify the difference between incrementing the pointer vs incrementing the value it points to
